author: potuz
content:
hey <@575994543527297055> can you corroborate here the issue with empty/full in some detail and how  an application of `should_build_on_full` was taken?

I assume that what you had is that you have a chain of full blocks, with the PTC voting somehow that they weren't timely and achieving quorum over this (which is already surprising) and also missing slots in between. 

So attesters attested for full explicitly because of the missed slots and attesters don't care about the payload being late (what I am mentioning above). Therefore head is always for  full in that chain's view. 

Then suddenly a CL block comes and builds on top of an earlier block, so that this CL block is not viewed as our head simply by running forkchoice. Then we run forkchoice and we find that our head is from a previous slot and is full, but when we go and count the PTC vote we see that there was consensus that it was late or not available. Thus we attempt to reorg the payload because of an incorrect application of `should_build_on_full`.

I assume this is the situation. But how in the hell did the chain get into a situation in which there is consensus among the PTC that the payloads weren't available or timely and still the chain kept building on full?

author: potuz
content: I just want confirmation that the above was the situation with Lodestar.

author: nflaig
content: I am also still confused how `should_build_on_full` managed to return `False` for `22903` but that's the only explanation I have right now and since we called this even after missed slots, we kept building on `EMPTY` even though `FULL` had much more weight, so we didn't even accept the own block we build as head

author: nflaig
content: so `lodestar-nethermind-2` proposed this block https://dora.glamsterdam-devnet-4.ethpandaops.io/slot/22903, the same node later build on top of that block https://dora.glamsterdam-devnet-4.ethpandaops.io/slot/22912, but on `EMPTY`, but looking at the weights via fork choice, it's `EMPTY=9504` and `FULL=15968` so it should build on full unless `should_build_on_full` was wrongly returning `False`

author: potuz
content: but do you have a block that included the PTC votes for that block 22903?

author: potuz
content: Either way there seems to be a bug, that PTC is actually achieving consensus on unavailable or empty

author: nflaig
content: wouldn't that only be the case if there was a block for 904?

author: potuz
content: yeah, there was no block produced? I'm not even looking at dora, if there's no block, do you have a forkchoice dump that includes them?

author: potuz
content: have the API people even agreed on a format for the forkchoice dump?

author: potuz
content: I think the only way to achieve actual consensus on this is to have nodes sending PTC attestations for blocks they haven't processed. Prysm for example could not have even voted for those blockroots since they could not have imported them as they lacked a parent payload

author: nflaig
content: I am not aware of anyone proposing a format, I just created https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/issues/576 a while ago

author: nflaig
content: I think <@575994543527297055> wanting to look into enhancing our forkchoice dump to make it easier to debug this

author: nflaig
content: we have our own `GET /eth/v0/debug/forkchoice` but that dumps our proto array, so this doesn't apply to prysm

author: potuz
content: If no one takes over the API issue by today I'll implement one in Prysm and open that implementation as an API pr. Similar to the builders endpoint that has me blocked still

author: nflaig
content: any proposal is appreciated, we can go with the prysm implementation as a proposal, people can complain if they don't like it and we can discuss on the PR and iterate on it

author: nflaig
content: generally for gloas it's hard to move some stuff because nobody has strong opinions or gives feedback on api stuff
